summary: Unknown recording source but likely decent; the show shines with a rare bust‑out of “The Greatest Story Ever Told” and the debut of “Easy to Love You”, plus an unusually cool drum jam.
review: Reviewers rave about this performance, noting that the band finally revived “The Greatest Story Ever Told” after a long hiatus and introduced the fresh “Easy to Love You”.  A standout drum‑into‑jam segment is highlighted as out‑of‑the‑ordinary for early‑’79.  No specific comments on the audio fidelity are provided, so the recording quality remains unclear.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed 4‑star review praises the musical set (rare and debut songs, strong drum jam) while offering no recording details; rating reflects a good show with unknown but likely acceptable audio.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Review does not mention sound clarity, mix or source type, so the technical quality cannot be assessed.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Review highlights a rare early‑’79 revival of “The Greatest Story Ever Told” and the fresh debut of “Easy to Love You”, with an unusually engaging drum jam that adds momentum to the set.

band_member_comments.Keys: Brent is credited with pushing “Easy to Love You” into the band’s rotation, suggesting strong keyboard involvement.
band_member_comments.Drums: The jam into drums is described as very cool and out of the ordinary for this period.
